Title: PoDPBT, a BAHD acyltransferase, catalyses the benzoylation in paeoniflorin biosynthesis in Paeonia ostii

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; clade V-i; conserved HXXXD motif; altered DFGGG/DFGWG-region motif; YPLAGR motif; QVTRLKCGG motif
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream modification step; terminal/late benzoylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a benzoyl group from benzoyl-CoA to 8-debenzoylpaeoniflorin at the C8 hydroxyl to form paeoniflorin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; first identified enzyme in the modification stage of paeoniflorin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Important medicinal compound with reported antidepression, antithrombosis, immunoregulation, and neuroprotection activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes paeoniflorin biosynthesis by catalysing conversion of 8-debenzoylpaeoniflorin to paeoniflorin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq coexpression/correlation analysis; qRT-PCR expression profiling across developmental stages and organs; recombinant protein expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with UPLC-MS product identification; kinetic analysis; virus-induced gene silencing in Paeonia ostii leaves; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in budding-stage leaves (S3), lower in bud expanding stage (S4) and flowering stage (S5); highest in stem among tested organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in budding-stage leaves (S3), lower in S4 and S5; highest in stem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf developmental stage (S3 budding, S4 bud expanding, S5 flowering); organ type; TRV-based silencing treatment for in vivo validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq on BGISEQ-500 from leaves at S3/S4/S5; qRT-PCR validation; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purified recombinant protein size was consistent with predicted 49.91 kD. In vitro assay produced paeoniflorin as a single product. Kinetic parameters with 8-debenzoylpaeoniflorin: Km = 1364.54 ± 166.28 µM; kcat = 0.54 ± 0.05 s^-1. Silencing reduced but did not abolish paeoniflorin, attributed to residual PoDPBT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for metabolic engineering or synthetic biology to reconstruct/boost paeoniflorin production; can be combined with additional predicted modification enzymes such as hydroxylases and glycosyltransferases once identified
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PoDPBT (Pos.gene30573) was the only benzoyltransferase candidate with strong expression and high correlation with paeoniflorin accumulation across leaf developmental stages. 2. Recombinant PoDPBT directly converted 8-debenzoylpaeoniflorin and benzoyl-CoA into paeoniflorin in vitro, confirmed by UPLC-MS. 3. Silencing PoDPBT in P. ostii leaves significantly decreased paeoniflorin content, validating its in vivo role in paeoniflorin biosynthesis.
